summary: Ukrainian FPV pilots are targeting Russian forces, including attempts to dismount them from horses before striking, while Russian operators from the Rubikon Center reported 104 targets destroyed on March 11, 2026, including strikes on Ukrainian personnel and fixed-wing UAVs. A Russian infantryman was killed in a bayonet charge against a Ukrainian FPV drone, marking a first in warfare. The "Yolka" air defense drone performed a kinetic intercept of a Ukrainian fixed-wing UAV in spring 2026, and an American MaxxPro armored vehicle of the Ukrainian Armed Forces was destroyed by a Russian fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" in Dobropole. Ukrainian forces are increasing use of "Molnia" strike and reconnaissance drones, which are difficult to counter due to unknown control frequencies, while the enemy is deploying FPV drones in rows of 3-5 against Mavic-type UAVs.
